subject: Discrete Random Variables
topic: §16 Expected Value of a Function of a Discrete Random Variable
question: An insurance policy pays 100 per day for up to 3 days of hospitalization and 50 per day for each day of hospitalization thereafter. The number of days of hospitalization, $X$, is a discrete random variable with probability function $p(k) = \begin{cases} \frac{6-k}{15} & k = 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 \\ 0 & \text{otherwise} \end{cases}$. Determine the expected payment for hospitalization under this policy.
